title: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Unix and Linux Platforms
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Unix and Linux Platforms
text: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Unix and Linux Platforms To install Uniface on Unix and Linux platforms run the installation program. After installation, you can configure Uniface to work with your preferred databases and web servers. The information provided in this collection is specific to Unix and Linux platforms. For information on configuring the Uniface Router, Uniface Server, databases, network access and on integrating with other technologies, see the Related information links. Related Links Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages. TCP Connector The TCP connector provides network connectivity via the TCP/IP protocol. It handles unsecured IPv4 and IPv6 connections. It also establishes the physical connection for secure communication using the TLS connector. TLS Connector The TLS network connector provides an encrypted network connection between the Uniface Router and its Uniface Servers and client applications. It can also be used by the UPOPMAIL component to connect to SMTP servers over TLS. Integrating with Other Technologies The Uniface architecture enables you to integrate Uniface and non-Uniface components in a single application, and to deploy the application on a large variety of platforms and technologies. Configuring the Uniface Router To configure the Uniface Router, you need to edit its assignment file, where you can define the Uniface Servers that the Uniface Router can start, and their configuration parameters. Configuring the Uniface Server You need to configure the Uniface Server so that it can find the databases and Uniface files, entities, and components that it needs. Was this topic helpful?
